title: viola cazorlensis
intro: Viola cazorlensis, also known as the Cazorla pansy, is a species of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae) endemic to the Cazorla mountain in Andalusia, Spain. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory problems and digestive issues. The health benefits of Viola cazorlensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats. Herbal preparations of Viola cazorlensis can be made by infusing the dried flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's leaves in tinctures and salves.
